Notice Of Petition And Motion, And Petition And Motion, To Compel Arbitration And For An Order Staying The Action And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-14-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANDREW J. BROWN, Esq. of the Law Offices of Andrew J. Brown (Tel: 619-501-6550) for plaintiff;
Attys. KEVIN S. REED, Esq. and MEREDITH M. SHAW, Esq. of Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, LLP (Tel: 212-849-7000) for defendant Skillz Platform, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SKILLZ PLATFORM, INC.'s Petition And Motion, To Compel Arbitration And For An Order Staying The Action.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

***** After the hearing, the court signs an Order and Statement of Decision denying defendant's motion to compel arbitration and for an order staying the action. (See signed order for complete ruling.) (302/RBU) *****

Defendant Skillz Platform, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash And To Stay Document Requests And Deposition Notice Or, In The Alternative, For A Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-14-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANDREW J. BROWN, Esq. of the Law Offices of Andrew J. Brown (Tel: 619-501-6550) for plaintiff;
Attys. KEVIN S. REED, Esq. and MEREDITH M. SHAW, Esq. of Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, LLP (Tel: 212-849-7000) for defendant Skillz Platform, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SKILLZ PLATFORM, INC.'s Motion To Quash And To Stay Document Requests And Deposition Notice Or, In The Alternative, For A Protective Order.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant SKILLZ PLATFORM, INC.'s motion to quash is granted. The court adopts the Judge Pro Tem's report and recommendation. The Judge Pro Tem correctly concluded that this action (including discovery) was stayed pending ruling on the motion to compel arbitration and stay pursuant to CCP section 1281.4. Any relief from that stay to conduct discovery and/or introduce live testimony ought to have been part of plaintiff's opposition to the motion to compel arbitration. In any event, the matter appears moot given the ruling to deny the motion to compel arbitration.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
